rules: Only follow up where: original email was relevant, no bounce, no reply, enough time passed (30+ days for May 14 batch, 14+ days for later), follow-up adds new value

entries.revised_followup: Hi, I reached out last month about a calculator widget. I've now built a specific debt snowball vs avalanche tool that would pair well with your debt payoff content — readers can compare both methods with their actual numbers. Want me to send the embed?
entries.reason: 30+ days passed. New, more relevant offer. Site is appropriate target (PF blog, DR ~65).

entries.original_failure: Generic, offer mismatch (blog focuses on saving/budgeting)
entries.revised_followup: Hi Michelle, I reached out last month. I've built a debt snowball vs avalanche calculator that might help your readers who are working through debt — side-by-side comparison with their real numbers. Free, no signup. Want me to send the one-line embed?
entries.reason: 30+ days. Better offer match. Smaller site, genuine PF blogger.

entries.revised_followup: Hi Jeremy, I emailed in May. I noticed your investing content uses specific return calculations — I can create an interactive version where readers plug in their own numbers instead of reading static examples. Would that be useful for any of your articles? No cost, no signup.
entries.reason: 30+ days. Better personalized angle — custom calculation offer. Good target.

entries.revised_followup: Hi, I reached out in May about a calculator. I realize I sent the wrong tool — I actually have a mortgage affordability calculator that's a much better fit for your readers. Shows exact home price based on income, debts, and down payment. Free embed, no signup. Want me to send the code?
entries.reason: 30+ days. Corrected offer — mortgage widget for mortgage site. Strong fit.

entries.original_failure: Good email but no reply — may have been missed
entries.revised_followup: Hi Paula, checking in on the mortgage affordability widget I shared — no pressure, just wanted to make sure it didn't get buried. Happy to answer any questions or adjust sizing. Let me know either way!
entries.reason: ~12 days passed. Good original email. Worth one short check-in.

entries.original_failure: No reply — may have been missed
entries.revised_followup: Hi Robert, just checking if the mortgage affordability widget I shared would be useful for your readers. Happy to answer questions. Let me know either way!
entries.reason: ~12 days. Personalized original. Worth one short follow-up.

entries.revised_followup: Hi ESI, following up on the debt snowball vs avalanche widget — no worries if it's not a fit, just wanted to check it didn't get lost. Let me know!
entries.reason: ~12 days. Good original pitch. One short follow-up.

entries.revised_followup: Hi Kristy, quick follow-up on the FIRE-friendly mortgage calculator widget — just checking it didn't get buried. Happy to answer questions!
entries.reason: ~12 days. Good personalized pitch. Short check-in.

do_not_follow_up: NerdWallet — wrong target, too large, own calculators
Dave Ramsey — proprietary calculators, too large
Calculator.net — competitor
Bankrate — own calculator suite
CalcXML — competitor (sells embeddable calculators)
SmartAsset — direct competitor
MortgageCalculator.org — competitor
Credit Karma — too large, own tools
Omni Calculator — world's largest calculator platform
Mortgage News Daily — wrong offer (compound interest for mortgage site)
HSH.com — own calculators, wrong offer
Money.com — too large, own calculators
Rocket Homes — lead-gen calculators, wrong target
Mint/Intuit — too large, corporate
YNAB — SaaS company, wrong contact
Investopedia — DR 92, own calculators, already contacted twice
BiggerPockets — DR 80+, contacted twice, support@ not editorial
ChooseFI — already contacted, no reply
All batch 2 mass sends to credit card/debt sites — too generic, no personalization
Can I Retire Yet — already contacted in batch 2, no reply, wait longer
